name: cssselect

metadata.name: cssselect
metadata.summary: cssselect parses CSS3 Selectors and translates them to XPath 1.0

documentation:
# cssselect: CSS Selectors for Python

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/cssselect.svg
:alt: PyPI Version
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/cssselect
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/cssselect.svg
:alt: Supported Python Versions
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/cssselect
```

```{image} https://github.com/scrapy/cssselect/actions/workflows/tests.yml/badge.svg
:alt: Tests
:target: https://github.com/scrapy/cssselect/actions/workflows/tests.yml
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/scrapy/cssselect/master.svg
:alt: Coverage report
:target: https://codecov.io/github/scrapy/cssselect?branch=master
```

**cssselect** is a BSD-licensed Python library to parse [CSS3 selectors] and
translate them to [XPath 1.0] expressions.

[XPath 1.0] expressions can be used in [lxml] or another XPath engine to find
the matching elements in an XML or HTML document.

Find the cssselect online documentation at <https://cssselect.readthedocs.io>.

Quick facts:

- Source, issues and pull requests [on GitHub](https://github.com/scrapy/cssselect)
- Releases [on PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/cssselect/)
- Install with `pip install cssselect`

[css3 selectors]: https://www.w3.org/TR/selectors-3/
[lxml]: https://lxml.de/
[xpath 1.0]: https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath/all/

modules.name: cssselect
documentation:
CSS Selectors based on XPath
============================

This module supports selecting XML/HTML elements based on CSS selectors.
See the `CSSSelector` class for details.


:copyright: (c) 2007-2012 Ian Bicking and contributors.
            See AUTHORS for more details.
:license: BSD, see LICENSE for more details.

modules.name: cssselect.xpath
documentation:
cssselect.xpath
===============

Translation of parsed CSS selectors to XPath expressions.


:copyright: (c) 2007-2012 Ian Bicking and contributors.
            See AUTHORS for more details.
:license: BSD, see LICENSE for more details.

methods.name: cssselect.xpath.XPathExpr.add_star_prefix
documentation:
Append '*/' to the path to keep the context constrained
to a single parent.

classes.name: cssselect.xpath.GenericTranslator

methods.name: cssselect.xpath.GenericTranslator.css_to_xpath
documentation:
Translate a *group of selectors* to XPath.

Pseudo-elements are not supported here since XPath only knows
about "real" elements.

```
param css

A group of selectors as a string.

param prefix

This string is prepended to the XPath expression for each selector.
The default makes selectors scoped to the context node’s subtree.

raises

 on invalid selectors,
 on unknown/unsupported selectors,
including pseudo-elements.

returns

The equivalent XPath 1.0 expression as a string.
```

methods.name: cssselect.xpath.GenericTranslator.selector_to_xpath
documentation:
Translate a parsed selector to XPath.

```
param selector

A parsed  object.

param prefix

This string is prepended to the resulting XPath expression.
The default makes selectors scoped to the context node’s subtree.

param translate_pseudo_elements

Unless this is set to True (as  does),
the  attribute of the selector
is ignored.
It is the caller's responsibility to reject selectors
with pseudo-elements, or to account for them somehow.

raises

 on unknown/unsupported selectors.

returns

The equivalent XPath 1.0 expression as a string.
```

methods.name: cssselect.xpath.GenericTranslator.xpath_pseudo_element
documentation:
Translate a pseudo-element.

Defaults to not supporting pseudo-elements at all,
but can be overridden by sub-classes.

methods.name: cssselect.xpath.GenericTranslator.xpath_element
documentation: Translate a type or universal selector.

methods.name: cssselect.xpath.GenericTranslator.xpath_descendant_combinator
documentation: right is a child, grand-child or further descendant of left

methods.name: cssselect.xpath.GenericTranslator.xpath_child_combinator
documentation: right is an immediate child of left

methods.name: cssselect.xpath.GenericTranslator.xpath_direct_adjacent_combinator
documentation: right is a sibling immediately after left

methods.name: cssselect.xpath.GenericTranslator.xpath_indirect_adjacent_combinator
documentation: right is a sibling after left, immediately or not

methods.name: cssselect.xpath.GenericTranslator.xpath_relation_descendant_combinator
documentation: right is a child, grand-child or further descendant of left; select left

methods.name: cssselect.xpath.GenericTranslator.xpath_relation_child_combinator
documentation: right is an immediate child of left; select left

methods.name: cssselect.xpath.GenericTranslator.xpath_relation_direct_adjacent_combinator
documentation: right is a sibling immediately after left; select left

methods.name: cssselect.xpath.GenericTranslator.xpath_relation_indirect_adjacent_combinator
documentation: right is a sibling after left, immediately or not; select left

methods.name: cssselect.xpath.GenericTranslator.xpath_scope_pseudo
documentation: CSS immediate children (CSS ":scope > div" to XPath "child::div" or "./div")
Works only at the start of a selector
Needed to get immediate children of a processed selector in Scrapy

methods.name: cssselect.xpath.GenericTranslator.pseudo_never_matches
documentation: Common implementation for pseudo-classes that never match.

Translator for "generic" XML documents.

Everything is case-sensitive, no assumption is made on the meaning
of element names and attribute names.

classes.name: cssselect.xpath.HTMLTranslator

methods.name: cssselect.xpath.HTMLTranslator.xpath_disabled_pseudo
documentation: Links are never visited, the implementation for :visited is the same

Translator for (X)HTML documents.

Has a more useful implementation of some pseudo-classes based on
HTML-specific element names and attribute names, as described in
the [HTML5 specification]. It assumes no-quirks mode.
The API is the same as {class}`GenericTranslator`.

```
param xhtml

If false (the default), element names and attribute names
are case-insensitive.
```

[html5 specification]: http://www.w3.org/TR/html5/links.html#selectors

modules.name: cssselect.parser
documentation:
cssselect.parser
================

Tokenizer, parser and parsed objects for CSS selectors.


:copyright: (c) 2007-2012 Ian Bicking and contributors.
            See AUTHORS for more details.
:license: BSD, see LICENSE for more details.

functions.name: cssselect.parser.ascii_lower
documentation: Lower-case, but only in the ASCII range.

functions.name: cssselect.parser.parse
documentation:
Parse a CSS *group of selectors*.

If you don't care about pseudo-elements or selector specificity,
you can skip this and use {meth}`~GenericTranslator.css_to_xpath`.

```
param css

A group of selectors as a string.

raises

 on invalid selectors.

returns

A list of parsed  objects, one for each
selector in the comma-separated group.
```

functions.name: cssselect.parser.parse_series
documentation:
Parses the arguments for :nth-child() and friends.

:raises: A list of tokens
:returns: :``(a, b)``

classes.name: cssselect.parser.SelectorError
documentation:
Common parent for {class}`SelectorSyntaxError` and
{class}`ExpressionError`.

You can just use `except SelectorError:` when calling
{meth}`~GenericTranslator.css_to_xpath` and handle both exceptions types.

classes.name: cssselect.parser.SelectorSyntaxError
documentation: Parsing a selector that does not match the grammar.

classes.name: cssselect.parser.Selector

methods.name: cssselect.parser.Selector.canonical
documentation: Return a CSS representation for this selector (a string)

methods.name: cssselect.parser.Selector.specificity
documentation:
Return the [specificity] of this selector as a tuple of 3 integers.

[specificity]: http://www.w3.org/TR/selectors/#specificity

Represents a parsed selector.

{meth}`~GenericTranslator.selector_to_xpath` accepts this object,
but ignores {attr}`pseudo_element`. It is the user’s responsibility
to account for pseudo-elements and reject selectors with unknown
or unsupported pseudo-elements.

classes.name: cssselect.parser.FunctionalPseudoElement
documentation:
Represents selector::name(arguments)

```
.. attribute:: name

    The name (identifier) of the pseudo-element, as a string.
```

```
.. attribute:: arguments

    The arguments of the pseudo-element, as a list of tokens.

    **Note:** tokens are not part of the public API,
    and may change between cssselect versions.
    Use at your own risks.
```

classes.name: cssselect.parser.SpecificityAdjustment
documentation:
Represents selector:where(selector_list)
Same as selector:is(selector_list), but its specificity is always 0

classes.name: cssselect.parser.Element
documentation:
Represents namespace|element

`None` is for the universal selector '*'
